description:
## Bug When the mobile collapsible menu opens in `hx-top-nav`, the logo and hamburger toggle button shift upward by ~8px. This creates a jarring visual jump. ## Root Cause The `.nav__bar` container uses `display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; align-items: center; min-height: var(--hx-top-nav-height)`. - **Menu closed (1 flex line):** The single line stretches to fill `min-height: 4rem`. Items center vertically in 4rem = nice spacing. - **Menu open (2 flex lines):** The collapsible div (`width: 100%`) wraps to a second line. Total content exceeds `min-height`, so the first line shrinks to its natural content height (~48px). Items now center in ~48px instead of 64px = visual shift upward. ## Proposed Fix Separate the always-visible bar row from the collapsible panel so they don't share flex-wrap space: ```html
...
``` Where `.nav__bar` gets `display: flex; align-items: center; min-height: var(--hx-top-nav-height);` without `flex-wrap`. Alternative lighter fix: Add a dedicated inner wrapper for the first row with its own `min-height` and keep the current DOM structure. ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Logo and toggle button do not shift position when mobile menu opens/closes - [ ] Collapsible panel still opens below the bar row - [ ] Desktop layout unchanged - [ ] All existing tests pass - [ ] Animation (if any) still works
title: fix: hx-top-nav mobile menu open causes nav bar content to shift vertically

This creates a jarring visual jump. ## Root Cause The `.nav__bar` container uses `display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; align-items: center; min-height: var(--hx-top-nav-height)`. - **Menu closed (1 flex line):** The single line stretches to fill `min-height: 4rem`. Items center vertically in 4rem = nice spacing. - **Menu open (2 flex lines):** The collapsible div (`width: 100%`) wraps to a second line. Total content exceeds `min-height`, so the first line shrinks to its natural content height (~48px). Items now center in ~48px instead of 64px = visual shift upward. ## Proposed Fix Separate the always-visible bar row from the collapsible panel so they don't share flex-wrap space: ```html
...
``` Where `.nav__bar` gets `display: flex; align-items: center; min-height: var(--hx-top-nav-height);` without `flex-wrap`. Alternative lighter fix: Add a dedicated inner wrapper for the first row with its own `min-height` and keep the current DOM structure. ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Logo and toggle button do not shift position when mobile menu opens/closes - [ ] Collapsible panel still opens below the bar row - [ ] Desktop layout unchanged - [ ] All existing tests pass - [ ] Animation (if any) still works
